::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
pacman:: p_load(sf, sfdep, tmap, tidyverse)
```
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
hunan <- st_read(dsn = "data/geospatial", 
                 layer = "Hunan")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
Reading layer `Hunan' from data source 
  `C:\dewschan\ISSS622-GAA\In_class_Ex\In_Class_Ex05\data\geospatial' 
  using driver `ESRI Shapefile'
Simple feature collection with 88 features and 7 fields
Geometry type: POLYGON
Dimension:     XY
Bounding box:  xmin: 108.7831 ymin: 24.6342 xmax: 114.2544 ymax: 30.12812
Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84
```


:::
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
hunan2012 <- read_csv("data/aspatial/Hunan_2012.csv")
```
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
hunan_GPDPC <- left_join(hunan,hunan2012)
```
:::



Step 1 : Deriving Queen's contiguity weights: Sfdep methods

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
wm_q <- hunan_GPDPC %>% 
  mutate (nb=st_contiguity(geometry),wt = st_weights (nb,style ="W"), .before=1)
```
:::



computing Global Moran'I

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
moranI <-global_moran(wm_q$GDPPC, wm_q$nb, wm_q$wt)

glimpse(moranI)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
List of 2
 $ I: num 0.301
 $ K: num 7.64
```


:::
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
global_moran_test(wm_q$GDPPC, wm_q$nb, wm_q$wt)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```

	Moran I test under randomisation

data:  x  
weights: listw    

Moran I statistic standard deviate = 4.7351, p-value = 1.095e-06
alternative hypothesis: greater
sample estimates:
Moran I statistic       Expectation          Variance 
      0.300749970      -0.011494253       0.004348351 
```


:::
:::

The P value is lower than the z 


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
set.seed(1234)
```
:::



this kind of analysis is faster then point pattern. 

Even running 999 simulation, the global moran is the same.  

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
global_moran_perm(wm_q$GDPPC, wm_q$nb, wm_q$wt, nsim= 99)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```

	Monte-Carlo simulation of Moran I

data:  x 
weights: listw  
number of simulations + 1: 100 

statistic = 0.30075, observed rank = 100, p-value < 2.2e-16
alternative hypothesis: two.sided
```


:::
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
Lisa<- wm_q %>%
  mutate(local_moran = local_moran( GDPPC, nb, wt, nsim= 99), .before =1) %>% unnest(local_moran)
```
:::




tmap_mode("plot")
map1 <-tm_shape(Lisa) +
  tm_fill("ii")
  tm_borders(alpha = 0.5)
  tm_view(set,zoom.limit= c(6,8)) +
  tm_layout(main.title = "local Moran's I of GDPPC
            main.title.size=2")
  
map2<- tm_shape(Lisa) +
  tm_fill("p_il", breaks(0.001,0.01, 0.05,1),)



Lisa_sig <- Lisa %>%
  filter(p_ii <0.05)
tmap_mode("plot")
tm_shape(Lisa)+
  tm_borders(alpha= 0,5)+
  tm_shape(Lisa_sig)+
  tm_fill("mean")+
  tm_borders(alpha= 0.4)




wm-idw <- hunan_GPPC %>%
  mutate(nb = st_contiguity(geometry), scale= 1, alpha= 1m ,belfoer =1 ))



HCSA <- HCS
